transcript: So when these 100 men are lined up and they're looking to the right and they're left, they know that out of the 100 here who begin the assault, only a handful will see the end of that day. Most of them would be dead and many of them would be worse than dead, handicapped, amputees. And yet, they will write their last letters to their families, to their parents, to their unborn children at times. They will heft their battle packs, cock up their rifles.
